top10.introHtml: Ranked by the cheap fun second car lens: working top condition first, then reliable model family, manual-preferred, mileage flexible. Data pulled June 13, 2026 from Cars.com (clean title + no-accidents filtered), CarGurus, and AutoTrader within 35 miles of Media, PA.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.8 has BMW's inline-six engine vs. the anemic 1.9L four — a fundamentally different driving experience. At $7,940 this is the cheapest proper-engine Z3 available within 35 miles. 75k miles is barely broken-in for an M52 inline-six. Only 15 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: Run the soft-top through a full cycle — check for torn fabric, stuck motor, or bent frame. Inspect headliner and rear carpet for water staining (common from aged top seals). Check for rear differential clunk. Verify coolant expansion tank isn't cracked (common failure). VANOS rattle on startup is wear indicator.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck fun car in the entire list — inline-six Z3 at under $8k, close to home.

picks.whyGood: Mid-engine, rear-wheel-drive roadster at 56,500 miles for $12,500 is exceptional — these have largely disappeared from the market. Manual-only (no automatic was offered), 1ZZ-FE is bulletproof. Fun-per-dollar ratio is off the charts.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top operation, check for cracks in rear window plastic. Inspect rear trunk/engine bay for any signs of overheating (rare but check). Spider bite: throttle response is tail-happy — ask for a test drive on a curved road. Verify no cracked exhaust manifold.
picks.bottomLine: Rarest and most driver-focused car on this list — mid-engine fun that you won't find again at this price.

picks.whyGood: Cheapest Boxster in the area at $11,250 with 74k miles. Mid-engine balance, proper sports car feel. Only 11 miles from Media. The 2.7L base Boxster is reliable; the IMS bearing issue is less severe on this engine than the S.
picks.whatToVerify: IMS bearing — verify if intermediate shaft bearing has been upgraded (Gen 1 986 cars have higher risk; ask for service records). Check for RMS (rear main seal) leak. Run top through full cycle. Listen for ticking at startup (IMS warning). Verify AOS (Air Oil Separator) condition.
picks.bottomLine: Closest Porsche to home, lowest price — get the IMS verified and this is a dream second car.

picks.whyGood: 39,382 miles on a 2008 roadster is extraordinary — someone barely drove this car. The Kappa platform (shared with Pontiac Solstice) is mechanically straightforward, fun rear-wheel-drive, and clean title/no accidents per Cars.com. Under $10k.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top operation and seal condition (sitting for long periods degrades rubber). Check for rodent nesting under hood (stored cars). Test all electrics thoroughly. Saturn parts availability is declining — source a source before buying.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest mileage roadster on the entire list by far — worth the 44-mile drive for a PPI.

picks.whyGood: R57 MINI Cooper at 52,000 miles with clean title, only 8 miles away. Modern infotainment, reliable B16 engine, fun go-kart handling. Good practical daily for a cheap second car. Private seller pricing.
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation (electric motor on R57 can fail — verify slow/hesitant movement), water pump (known failure on N16 engines), check for timing chain rattle. Verify no oil leaks from valve cover.
picks.bottomLine: Modern, practical, close, and the right price — easiest daily-driver fun car on the list.

picks.whyGood: E93 328i — the last 'analog' BMW 3-Series convertible, with a hardtop that stows automatically. 80k miles on a well-maintained N52 engine is nothing. 15 miles away. Great balance of luxury, fun, and reliability.
picks.whatToVerify: Hardtop retraction mechanism (hydraulic lines can develop slow leaks), VANOS operation, valve cover gasket (common leak), check for signs of overheating in E90-family cars. Verify power steering fluid reservoir condition.
picks.bottomLine: Practical hardtop convertible with BMW inline-six — most weather-versatile fun car here.

picks.whyGood: Only 45,587 miles on a 20-year-old car is exceptional — clearly garaged and lightly driven. Clean title, no accidents. 1.8T turbocharged engine is strong. Audi cabriolet is elegant and well-built.
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation (B6 Cabriolet hydraulic top motors are expensive to replace), coolant system (thermostat, water pump on 1.8T), timing belt service history (critical on 1.8T — $800+ job if neglected), check for oil sludge (1.8T known issue on low-synthetic-oil cars).
picks.bottomLine: Lowest mileage Audi cabriolet on the list — buy it if the top works and timing belt is current.

picks.whyGood: R57 MINI Cooper S — supercharged (not turbo) B14 engine, very spirited and characterful. 65k miles at $8,452 is a good price. The S trim adds meaningful performance over the base Cooper.
picks.whatToVerify: Supercharger belt and tensioner condition, water pump (known failure), timing chain stretch (R52/R57 S models), top fabric and frame condition, check for rust on underbody.
picks.bottomLine: Cheapest Cooper S convertible in the area — fun supercharged motor at a fair price.

picks.whyGood: 44,000 miles on a 30-year-old Mustang GT is incredible — clearly a kept car. SN-95 GT with 5.0L V8 (last year), clean title, no accidents, private seller. This is a future collectible.
picks.whatToVerify: Top frame integrity (SN-95 convertible top mounts are known rust points at the A-pillars and rear quarter), check lower rockers and frame rails for rust, T-5 transmission third-gear synchro test (slow shift to 3rd), rear axle clunk, brake booster condition.
picks.bottomLine: 44k miles on a Fox-era 5.0 Mustang GT — numbers almost never seen; get the PPI done.

picks.whyGood: Only 4 miles from Media — the closest car on this list. 2016 Audi A3 Cabriolet at $11,999 is solid value; MQ200 transmission and 2.0T engine are well-proven at this mileage. Modern tech (MMI, Audi Connect) and all-weather AWD-lite handling.
picks.whatToVerify: DSG (S-Tronic) service history — mechatronic unit oil change at 40k intervals is critical. Top operation, check seal at rear window. Verify no DSG shudder on low-speed engagement. Carbon buildup on direct-inject intake (common on EA888).
picks.bottomLine: Closest car to home, modern Audi, solid price — easiest pick if you want something you can daily without drama.

mustangs.introHtml: All Ford Mustang convertibles within 35 miles of Media, PA between $7k–$15k, ranked GT-first per the Mustang buyer lens. SN-95 GTs dominate the value picks this week.

picks.whyGood: Last year of the original 5.0L V8 in the SN-95 body — 44,000 miles is museum-quality low. Clean title and no accidents per Cars.com. Private seller means no dealer markup.
picks.whatToVerify: Top frame at A-pillar mounts, rocker rust, T-5 third-gear synchro test, 8.8 rear axle clunk, check brake booster, inspect convertible rear seat water staining.
picks.bottomLine: Best Mustang on the list by far — 44k miles on a 5.0 GT you won't find again at this price.

picks.whyGood: 55k miles SN-95 GT for $10,495 — nearly identical to rank 1 but with slightly more miles and a dealer. GT trim, proper V8, low mileage for the year.
picks.whatToVerify: Same checklist as rank 1: top mounts, T-5 synchro, rear axle, convertible top seal at rear window. Ask dealer for any rust treatment history.
picks.bottomLine: Backup to rank 1 if that one sells — equally desirable SN-95 GT at the same price.

picks.whyGood: Fox body 5.0 GT convertible — the classic. $9,500 for a clean-title, no-accidents Fox with 98k miles is strong value. 16 miles away. These are genuinely collectible now.
picks.whatToVerify: Fox body convertible top frame at windshield header, rocker panels (major rust zone), check 302 for valve stem seals (blue smoke), T-5 shifter slop, fuel injector rail condition.
picks.bottomLine: Classic Fox body at a classic price — the most character of any Mustang on this list.

picks.whyGood: S197 V6 at 64k miles, clean title, no accidents — the modern chassis is solid and the 3.7L V6 makes 305hp. Better daily driver than the old 5.0s. 42 miles is the only real downside.
picks.whatToVerify: Rear deck lid seal (common water leak on S197 convertibles), IRS on GT500-equipped cars doesn't apply here (solid rear), check for soft-top motor noise. Verify no previous hail or parking damage on roof.
picks.bottomLine: Most practical modern Mustang convertible at a fair price — good choice if you want reliability over character.

picks.whyGood: S550 Premium body style — the best-looking modern Mustang convertible. 141k miles is high but the Coyote 5.0L (if V8) or EcoBoost are proven to 200k+. Dealer listing with recent price likely negotiable.
picks.whatToVerify: Verify V8 vs. EcoBoost (huge difference in fun factor). Check for top mechanism binding, rear deck seal leak, transmission fluid condition (10-speed auto can be problematic at high miles). Ask for full service history.
picks.bottomLine: Newest Mustang by far — worth it if it's a V8 with documented service; negotiate hard on price given mileage.
